description_long: This guard rail is constructed from weathered, grayish wood, featuring three horizontal rails supported by four vertical posts. The wood displays a rough, natural grain texture, with slight warping and irregularities that give it a rustic appearance. The posts are evenly spaced and extend slightly above the top rail, providing a sturdy framework. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared off, with no visible end treatments or additional safety features. There are no reflective markings or metal components visible, emphasizing its simple, utilitarian design. This type of guard rail is commonly found in parks, rural roadsides, or as a boundary in urban green spaces.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden guard rail with three horizontal rails and four vertical posts, featuring a rough, natural texture and blunt ends.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the guard rail, with the posts stacked in line and minimal rail visibility.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full length of the guard rail, with three horizontal rails and four vertical posts clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the narrow edge and the posts aligned vertically.
description_view_3: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the three rails and four posts from the opposite side.
